headline: two notes, one truth

cols.head: ONE CANONICAL RECORD
cols.body: 'api 504s: cap migration concurrency.' canonical, fresh. the rest superseded.

sub: One store. Every agent reads it before it writes.

headline: the agent didn't lie, it read half the doc
sub: It read half a 40-page doc and answered with full confidence. The fix: scope the context, and log how much of the doc made the window.

headline: trovex didn't help us at first
sub: Garbage docs in, confident garbage out. Fix your docs first; the tool only serves what you actually wrote.

headline: when do you stop reading every diff?
sub: Three clean PRs in a row is the signal. You start trusting the fleet and spot-check, instead of gating everything.

headline: the model isn't the variable, your codebase is
sub: Stanford, 100k+ developers across 600+ companies. The gain shrinks the moment it meets a real codebase.

headline: most AI strategy is a procurement plan
sub: Not an operating-model change. The real questions: who owns agent output, and how does it get reviewed?
